name: seed-commit-log
purpose: Backfills .claude/state/commit-log.jsonl from git history; called by check-session-gaps.js when the log is missing or empty.
notes: CJS with guarded require pattern. Two modes: bulk-seed (count arg, default 50, max 500) and --sync (incremental append). Writes .claude/state/commit-log.jsonl atomically via safe-fs tmp-then-rename. SESSION_CONTEXT.md is read for session counter via string parsing (no regex, SonarCloud S5852 compliance). Runtime caller: check-session-gaps.js (spawns it as subprocess). JASON-OS status: NOT PRESENT but high-value port candidate — portable, no SoNash business logic.

name: session-end-commit
purpose: Auto-commits and pushes SESSION_CONTEXT.md at session end; invoked via npm run session:end.
notes: CJS. Forked to JASON-OS with one enhancement: JASON-OS version uses process.execPath (not hardcoded 'node') and resolves log-override.js via absolute REPO_ROOT path for parity with runGit cwd pattern. JASON-OS also supports both 'Uncommitted Work: Yes/No' inline-bold format AND '## Uncommitted Work\n<value>' D12 heading format via uncommittedWorkPattern regex (SoNash version only supports inline-bold). SoNash version: 272 lines, uses execFileSync('node', ['scripts/log-override.js', ...]) with relative paths. JASON-OS version: 284 lines, uses process.execPath + absolute path. Both commit ONLY SESSION_CONTEXT.md via git commit --only flag. Both set SKIP_DOC_INDEX_CHECK and SKIP_DOC_HEADER_CHECK env vars. JASON-OS version is the more correct/portable version.

name: setup-cli-tools
purpose: Deploys CLI tool configs (delta, starship, zoxide) from repo tool-configs/ to user home directories; writes to ~/.config and ~/.bashrc.
sanitize_fields: tool list — starship.toml content is SoNash-specific
notes: Bash (none module_system). 74 lines. set -euo pipefail. Idempotent — each tool section is guarded by command -v check. Writes to machine scope (~/.config, ~/.bashrc). The sanitize point is the starship.toml config itself (SoNash-specific prompt layout). The bash structure for deploying config files is fully portable. No test coverage (bash script, expected). Compare to install-cli-tools.sh (D6c pos 41) which uses winget/curl for installation — this script is for post-install config deployment. Complementary scripts, not duplicates.

name: suggest-pattern-automation
purpose: Analyzes AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md to find code patterns that could be automated in check-pattern-compliance.js but aren't yet; optionally adds them.
notes: ESM (mixed — uses createRequire for safe-fs). 422 lines. Hard-couples to docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md and scripts/check-pattern-compliance.js — both SoNash-specific files. The pattern extraction logic (Wrong:/Bad:/INCORRECT: prefix scanning, section regex) is generic but the target files make this not-portable. Uses sanitizeError from ./lib/sanitize-error.js (note: .js not .cjs extension). Secret/credential redaction pipeline in sanitizeCodeForLogging function. No tool_deps — pure Node.js file analysis.

name: surface-lessons-learned
purpose: Session-start helper that searches docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md for relevant past issues based on current work context (modified files, keywords).
notes: ESM (mixed — createRequire for load-config). 411 lines. Hard-depends on scripts/config/load-config.js for skill-config.json (TOPIC_ALIASES). Also hard-couples to docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md and SoNash-specific topic aliases (firebase, auth, roadmap, session, etc.). Uses execSync for git diff (note: execSync not execFileSync — shell: true by default, less secure than execFileSync). Called by session-begin workflow for pre-session context injection. not-portable due to load-config and SoNash-specific topic structure.

name: sync-warnings-ack
purpose: Syncs lastCleared timestamp in hook-warnings-ack.json by checking if every current warning type has a per-type acknowledgment; keeps the statusline unread-count accurate.
notes: CJS strict mode. 136 lines. Idempotent — safe to run multiple times. No shells_out. Reads .claude/state/hook-warnings-log.jsonl and .claude/state/hook-warnings-ack.json; writes ack.json via safeWriteFileSync. Guards both files with lstatSync symlink check before reading. 2MB guard on JSONL file. Companion to append-hook-warning.js (D6a) and resolve-hook-warnings.js (D6c). Forms the third script in the hook-warning trio. Uses validatePathInDir from security-helpers. No test file found (genuine gap). JASON-OS status: NOT PRESENT. High-value port — needed for statusline integration.

name: test-hook-gates
purpose: Gate test harness: simulates PreToolUse/PostToolUse/PostToolUseFailure stdin payloads and invokes hook scripts directly without needing a Claude Code session restart.
notes: CJS. 400 lines. Uses spawn() (async) to invoke hook scripts. Builds synthetic stdin payload matching Claude Code hook event schema. Supports --hook, --event, --tool, --input, --env flags. Exit code labels: 0=PASS, 2=BLOCK. Uses sanitize-error.cjs (explicit .cjs extension). The hook test harness is fully generic — no SoNash business logic. Depends on hook scripts being present at specified paths. No test file found. JASON-OS status: NOT PRESENT. High-value port — essential for hook development/testing without session restart.

name: test-hooks
purpose: Hook health test suite: validates all Claude Code hooks via syntax validation, basic execution, and behavioral tests; run via npm run hooks:test.
sanitize_fields: HOOK_TESTS object — hook names and test inputs reference SoNash-specific hooks
notes: CJS. 674 lines. The HOOK_TESTS map embeds all SoNash hook names and their expected behaviors (firestore-write-block, component-size-check, pattern-check, etc.). The test framework itself (syntax check via node --check, execution harness, expect-ok pattern) is portable. The sanitize point is the HOOK_TESTS constant — replace with JASON-OS hook names and test cases. Uses path containment validation (correct regex /^\.\.[\\//]|$/ pattern). Has test file test-hooks.test.ts in tests/scripts/. JASON-OS status: NOT PRESENT but framework is valuable.

name: update-readme-status
purpose: Updates README.md project status section from ROADMAP.md milestone data; run manually or from npm scripts.
notes: ESM (mixed — createRequire for safe-fs). 601 lines. Reads ROADMAP.md (SoNash-specific milestone structure) and writes README.md. The ROADMAP.md parsing logic is tightly coupled to SoNash's specific heading/table format. not-portable due to ROADMAP.md dependency. Uses sanitizeError from ./lib/sanitize-error.js (.js extension). Has test coverage in tests/scripts/.

name: validate-audit
purpose: Post-audit validation for JSONL audit findings: validates against FALSE_POSITIVES.jsonl, evidence requirements, confidence scoring, and duplicate detection.
notes: ESM (mixed — createRequire for parse-jsonl-line). 1019 lines. Validates the SoNash audit JSONL schema (REQUIRED_FIELDS_BY_SEVERITY S0-S3, VALID_CONFIDENCES, VALID_TOOL_CONFIRMATIONS). Depends on docs/audits/FALSE_POSITIVES.jsonl and docs/audits/single-session/ directory structure — SoNash-specific. Two test files: validate-audit.test.ts AND validate-audit-s0s1.test.ts (split test suite). Uses execSync (not execFileSync) for cross-tool calls — less secure pattern. not-portable due to SoNash-specific audit schema and directory layout.

name: validate-canon-schema
purpose: Validates CANON-*.jsonl files against the SoNash multi-AI aggregator schema (canonical_id, category, title, severity, effort, files fields).
notes: ESM (mixed — createRequire for parse-jsonl-line). 514 lines. Validates SoNash-specific CANON-XXXX ID format (4-digit), S0-S3 severity, E0-E3 effort, CONFIRMED/SUSPECTED/NEW/TRACKED_ELSEWHERE status. Also detects alternative ID formats (F-XXX, PERF-XXX, CANON-R-XXX, CANON-D-XXX, CANON-P-XXX) for better error messages. Path containment via realpathSync. not-portable — specific to SoNash multi-AI review CANON schema. Uses sanitizeError from ./lib/sanitize-error.js (.js extension).

name: validate-hook-manifest
purpose: Validates bi-directional sync between scripts/config/hook-checks.json and the actual .husky/pre-commit and .husky/pre-push hook scripts; ensures every manifest check has a bash implementation.
sanitize_fields: PRE_COMMIT_PATTERNS, PRE_PUSH_PATTERNS — hardcoded check IDs reference SoNash-specific checks
notes: CJS strict. 438 lines. The manifest validation concept (JSON contract vs bash implementation) is highly portable. The PRE_COMMIT_PATTERNS and PRE_PUSH_PATTERNS arrays embed SoNash-specific check IDs (gitleaks, pattern-compliance, audit-s0s1, skill-validation, etc.) — these are the sanitize_fields. The sanitizeError from security-helpers is used for CJS compatibility (not sanitize-error.cjs or sanitize-error.js). No test file found (genuine gap). JASON-OS status: NOT PRESENT. Concept is portable; only the check lists need updating.

name: validate-phase-completion
purpose: CI enforcement script: validates INTEGRATED_IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md phase completions have documented acceptance criteria and accomplished-what sections.
notes: ESM. 156 lines. Hardcodes path to docs/archive/completed-plans/INTEGRATED_IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md — a SoNash-specific archived planning doc. The path is already in docs/archive/ which signals this was a one-time CI gate for a specific plan. Status classified as deprecated because the plan is archived. The validation logic (## Phase header parsing, '### What Was Accomplished' check, [x] acceptance criteria) is generic but the target doc is obsolete. Has test coverage in tests/scripts/.

name: validate-skill-config
purpose: Validates skill and command configuration files: checks YAML frontmatter, title heading, required sections, file references, and deprecated patterns.
sanitize_fields: REQUIRED_SKILL_MD_SECTIONS — currently SoNash defaults; REQUIRED_SECTIONS and DEPRECATED_PATTERNS from scripts/config/skill-config.json
notes: CJS. 341 lines. Scans .claude/commands/ and .claude/skills/ directories. Uses loadConfigWithRegex from scripts/config/load-config for skill-config.json (requiredSections, deprecatedPatterns). The core validation logic (frontmatter parsing including block scalars, section presence checks) is portable. Sanitize point is the skill-config.json content — JASON-OS can supply its own. The custom YAML block-scalar parser (handles >-, >, |-, | markers and indented continuation lines) is noteworthy quality. Has test coverage in tests/scripts/.

name: verify-enforcement
purpose: Reads learning-routes.jsonl, runs verification tests for enforced entries, and updates status to verified or flags for repair.
notes: CJS strict. 553 lines. Reads .claude/state/learning-routes.jsonl (SoNash-specific state file). Runs enforcement test scripts referenced in learning-routes entries via execFileSync(process.execPath, [testPath]). Repo boundary check via realpathSync + /^\.\.[\\//]|$/ pattern (correct). Has fallback implementations for safe-fs, sanitizeError, and readJsonl if unavailable. Uses withLock from safe-fs for concurrent write safety. Has test file scripts/__tests__/verify-enforcement.test.js (JavaScript, not TypeScript — in co-located __tests__ not tests/scripts/). not-portable: depends on SoNash learning-routes.jsonl schema and enforcement test structure.

name: verify-skill-usage
purpose: Verifies expected skills/agents were invoked based on session activity log; warns if code-reviewer, security-auditor, or test-engineer were skipped when triggered.
sanitize_fields: USAGE_RULES — skill names (code-reviewer, security-auditor, test-engineer, systematic-debugging) are SoNash-specific; EXCLUDE_PATHS patterns may need updating
notes: CJS. 255 lines. Reads .claude/session-activity.jsonl for file_write/file_edit events. USAGE_RULES map file patterns to skill names — sanitize_fields covers these. The framework (spawnSync git for repo root, event-based triggering, severity warnings, exit 1 in strict mode) is portable. The skill names in USAGE_RULES match the SoNash .claude/agents/ roster. Has test coverage in tests/scripts/. JASON-OS status: NOT PRESENT. High-value port once skill roster is established.

name: generate-documentation-index
purpose: Scans all markdown files and generates DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md with categories, descriptions, references, and dependencies.
notes: GAP FILL (D6b coverage gap). ESM native (.mjs extension forces ESM regardless of package.json). 1147 lines. Uses loadConfig('doc-generator-config') for category definitions, exclude patterns, output path — all SoNash-specific. Shells out to git for file metadata. not-portable due to doc-generator-config.json content (SoNash category taxonomy). Has test coverage in tests/scripts/. This is the largest script in the entire root scripts/ directory. Position 34 in alphabetical sort — fell in D6b/D6c numbering gap.

name: generate-skill-registry
purpose: Scans .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/ directories, parses SKILL.md frontmatter, and writes a structured JSON registry to scripts/config/skill-registry.json.
sanitize_fields: SKILLS_DIRS — includes .agents/skills/ which does not exist in JASON-OS; output path scripts/config/skill-registry.json
notes: GAP FILL (D6b coverage gap). CJS. 164 lines. Custom YAML frontmatter parser (O(n) string walk, no regex — SonarCloud S5852 compliance). Scans both .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/. sanitize_fields: JASON-OS has no .agents/skills/ — update SKILLS_DIRS to .claude/skills/ only. Has test coverage in tests/scripts/. Position 37 in alphabetical sort.

name: generate-test-registry
purpose: Scans for all 8 validation source types and outputs data/ecosystem-v2/test-registry.jsonl; supports --check-coverage mode.
notes: GAP FILL (D6b coverage gap). CJS strict. 796 lines. Scans for 8 SoNash-specific source types including ecosystem audit checkers, test protocols, npm validators, CI steps. Writes to data/ecosystem-v2/test-registry.jsonl — SoNash data dir. Has .test-baseline.json diff support. Uses sanitize-error.cjs (explicit .cjs). not-portable: deeply embedded in SoNash ecosystem directory structure. No test file found. Position 38 in alphabetical sort.

name: hook-analytics
purpose: Hook and agent observability report: reads override-log.jsonl, commit-failures.jsonl, and agent-invocations.jsonl; produces threshold-based recommendations with --since filtering.
notes: GAP FILL (D6b coverage gap). CJS. 300 lines. Reads .claude/state/ JSONL files (override-log.jsonl, commit-failures.jsonl, agent-invocations.jsonl). Default --since=2026-03-12 (post pre-existing ban date — SoNash-specific default, easily changed). Uses spawnSync for git rev-parse only. 2MB guard per file. The analytics logic (override frequency, false positive indicators, keyword scanning, threshold recommendations) is fully generic — portable once the default --since date is updated. Has test coverage in tests/scripts/. High-value port candidate. Position 39 in alphabetical sort.

name: hook-report
purpose: Post-hook summary report generator: reads a checks temp file and produces a formatted report with pass/fail status, durations, and remediation suggestions per check.
sanitize_fields: CHECK_SCOPES object — check names and descriptions reference SoNash-specific checks
notes: GAP FILL (D6b coverage gap). CJS. 249 lines. The CHECK_SCOPES map (secrets-scan, eslint, pattern-compliance, firestore-write-block, etc.) is the sanitize_fields point. The report generation framework (reads check|status|duration_ms lines from tmpfile, formats table, optionally persists to .claude/state/last-hook-report.md via --persist) is portable. Uses sanitize-error.cjs. Called from .husky/pre-commit and .husky/pre-push with a temp file arg. No test file found. Position 40 in alphabetical sort. JASON-OS status: NOT PRESENT.
